title: Nature of liability in negligence
summary: 9084 Tort — negligence as civil wrong, fault principle, and three-part test overview.
learningObjectives: Define negligence and contrast with criminal liability.
Outline duty, breach, and damage framework.
Explain compensatory purpose of tort damages.
Evaluate role of negligence in modern tort law.

simpleExplanation.title: Careless harm and compensation
simpleExplanation.summary: Negligence imposes liability when D owes a duty, breaches it, and causes foreseeable damage — the dominant tort for personal injury claims.
steps: Civil wrong — claimant sues for compensation.
Fault-based — D must be at fault.

sections.content: **Nature of negligence** (4.1.1) opens Paper 4. Preview **duty → breach → causation** structure.
